idea-plugin.name: HubDev Manager
idea-plugin.description:
HubDev Manager — HubDev Integration for PhpStorm

Manage your HubDev local development sites directly from the IDE. Auto-detects the HubDev installation (including the legacy devhub.exe name on Windows), shows the current project's site status from the central sites.yml, and drives the hubdev CLI to link, unlink, start, stop, and secure sites — all without leaving PhpStorm.

Features

  • Auto-detection — Finds HubDev on Windows (devhub.exe / hubdev), macOS, and Linux
  • Site status — Reads ~/.devhub/config/sites.yml to show domain, PHP version, database, and mode
  • Site linking — Link/unlink the current project via the HubDev CLI (site:link / site:unlink)
  • Activation — Start/stop a site's Caddy route (site:start / site:stop)
  • SSL management — Renew certificates (site:secure)
  • Status bar widget — Link status at a glance
  • Tool window panel — Full management UI in the right sidebar
  • Smart notifications — Prompts to link on project open
  • Reactive updates — UI refreshes when sites.yml changes externally

Quick Start

  1. Open a Laravel project in PhpStorm
  2. The plugin auto-detects HubDev and checks if the project is registered
  3. If not linked, a notification offers to Link Now
  4. Click Link Site to register it with HubDev

Access the tool window via View → Tool Windows → HubDev Manager or the HubDev icon in the right sidebar. Actions also available under Tools → HubDev.

action.description: Open the HubDev Manager tool window
Open this site in the default browser
